classification_evidence: The change binds existing token-budget, pre-turn-check, and cost-reporting specs into ADLC Loop Contracts, action admission, workflow state, maturity reports, and agent guidance.
The change affects local schemas, CLI/MCP commands, fixtures, skills, and docs rather than an application runtime feature.

supported.claim: ADLC already has Loop Contract schemas, action validation, required-test evidence, maturity reports, workflow progress/control state, and assisted-loop no-overclaim behavior.
ADLC already has token-budget, pre-turn-check, cost-reporting, stop-reason, and session-state specs, but loop maturity does not require budget evidence for self-autonomous claims.
The loop-engineering strategy review identifies budget binding as the highest-value missing integration before claiming self-autonomous loop maturity.

supported_claims: Budget guards should become first-class evidence for LLM-backed Loop Contracts.
Missing budget evidence should block self_autonomous verdicts while preserving assisted_loop compatibility.
Budget enforcement should reuse existing token-budget and pre-turn-check specs rather than adding a second cost-control framework.
suspect_phrases: rewrite the ADLC runner as a scheduler
make every deterministic task carry budget fields
claim global self-autonomy after adding budget docs
implement provider billing integration in this slice

section_policy.reason: The change closes a specific loop-engineering strategy gap.
The user asked for end-to-end implementation tasks.
Schema, CLI, MCP, maturity, and contract-test surfaces must all be validated.
Planner, codegen context, Eval Council, and LLM security skills must consume budget evidence.
Budget guards become an implementation interface between Loop Contract, token-budget artifacts, action admission, maturity audit, and workflow state.
Self-autonomous loop claims need a no-overclaim production gate when budget evidence is missing, exhausted, or stale.

primary_verifier.rationale: The decomposition must remain schema-valid and readiness-clean before implementation starts.
primary_verifier.expected_failure_mode: loop budget guard decomposition missing or readiness blocked

secondary_verifiers.rationale: The decomposition must satisfy the strict ADLC Build Brief schema.

verification_spec.scope_note: This validates the plan artifact, not the implementation.

enterprise_readiness_contract.production_grade_target: Make token and cost budget evidence a first-class Loop Contract guard: LLM-backed loops declare budget refs, action admission checks projected spend before continuing, maturity reports show budget status, and self_autonomous claims are blocked when budget evidence is missing, stale, alerting, or exhausted.
enterprise_readiness_contract.backward_compatibility: Existing Build Briefs, deterministic tasks, Loop Contracts without autonomy claims, workflow states, and emitter payloads must continue to validate. Budget guard fields are optional unless a workflow claims self_autonomous or delegates an LLM-backed loop action.
enterprise_readiness_contract.forward_compatibility: Budget artifacts should reuse docs/schemas/token-budget.schema.json and docs/specs/pre-turn-check.md so future runtime adapters can connect real provider usage without changing Loop Contract semantics.
failure_mode_coverage: Runaway loops are covered by projected-token budget checks, hard-stop behavior, and structured budget_exhausted stop reasons.
Silent cost drift is covered by maturity-report budget_status evidence and workflow-state budget summaries.
Overclaimed autonomy is covered by downgrading self_autonomous requests when budget evidence is missing, stale, warning, alert, or exhausted.
Compatibility drift is covered by optional budget fields and validation fixtures for both budgeted and legacy assisted-loop contracts.
definition_of_done: Loop Contract, Loop Action, Loop Maturity Report, workflow-state, and token-budget aliases support budget guard evidence without breaking existing fixtures.
ADLC exposes deterministic loop-budget-check CLI and MCP surfaces that implement pre-turn budget decisions.
loop-action-validate can use budget evidence to reject or route budget-exhausted LLM actions before execution.
loop-maturity-audit emits budget_status and blocks self_autonomous when budget evidence is absent or unhealthy.
Planner, codegen-context, Eval Council, LLM security, README, and agent-native docs tell agents when budget guards are required.
Contract tests, CLI tests, schema validation, Build Brief readiness, and Graphify update/query pass end to end.
A follow-on tech-debt cleanup pass can run with implementation evidence and no broad unresolved generated churn.
enterprise_readiness_contract.compliance_posture: No external billing provider, account credential, secret, or production mutation is introduced. The slice uses local token-budget artifacts, cost estimates, workflow state, schema validation, and CLI/MCP contracts.
production_invariant_coverage: idempotency: budget checks are deterministic for the same token-budget artifact, projected action cost, and thresholds.
persistence: budget evidence is stored as repo-local fixtures or workflow/session state refs, not only in chat.
observability: CLI, workflow state, and maturity reports expose budget status, thresholds, projected tokens, and stop reason.
sensitive_data: budget reports store token and cost totals, not prompts, API keys, provider tokens, or raw billing data.
downgrade: unhealthy or missing budget evidence downgrades self_autonomous claims rather than blocking assisted_loop usage.

sections.1_context: ADLC already has Loop Contracts, action admission, required-test evidence, workflow progress/control state, maturity audit, and compound-learning refs. The local loop-engineering review identified one remaining production gap before stronger autonomous-loop claims: token and dollar budget evidence is documented in separate specs, but it is not yet bound into Loop Contracts, action validation, maturity reports, or no-overclaim gates.
sections.2_scope_and_constraints: In scope: budget guard semantics in loop-system docs, schema extensions, budget fixtures, token-budget schema aliasing, loop-budget-check CLI/MCP, action-admission budget checks, maturity-report budget_status, workflow-state budget summaries, planner/codegen/eval/security guidance, README/agent-native docs, and tests. Out of scope: provider billing APIs, actual hosted scheduler implementation, broad refactors unrelated to loop budget controls, or forcing budget artifacts onto deterministic docs/lint/build-validation work.
sections.3_repo_findings: Current ADLC evidence: README states assisted_loop by default and exposes loop-test-selection, loop-action-validate, and loop-maturity-audit. docs/specs/token-budgets.md, pre-turn-check.md, cost-reporting.md, token-budget.schema.json, and session-state.schema.json already define budgets. scripts/adlc_runtime/cli.py currently scores loop maturity from contract, state, tests, and action evidence, but not budget evidence. The next slice should bridge these existing surfaces rather than add a parallel budget framework.
sections.4_architecture: Add a Loop Budget Guard overlay. The Loop Contract may declare budget_guard refs and hard-stop behavior. The Loop Action may declare projected budget estimate. A new loop-budget-check command reads a token-budget artifact and projected action cost, then emits proceed, warning, wrap_up, or blocked with stop_reason budget_exhausted. loop-action-validate can consume the budget check before admitting an action. loop-maturity-audit includes budget_status and blocks self_autonomous unless budget evidence is healthy and current. Workflow state and resume output surface budget status for continuing loops.

5_security_review.risk: Budget reports could accidentally persist prompts, credentials, or provider account details.
5_security_review.mitigation: Budget artifacts store totals, thresholds, model IDs, and evidence refs only; docs and skills must explicitly forbid raw prompts, API keys, bearer tokens, and billing account identifiers.

5_security_review.risk: Budget checks could be bypassed by omitting budget evidence from a self_autonomous loop.
5_security_review.mitigation: Maturity audit must downgrade or block self_autonomous when budget evidence is missing, stale, warning, alert, or exhausted.

5_security_review.risk: Budget guard fields could make existing deterministic tasks noisy or brittle.
5_security_review.mitigation: Budget fields stay optional unless the task delegates an LLM-backed loop action or claims self_autonomous maturity.

sections.6_observability_slo: Every active budget guard should leave machine-readable evidence: token budget artifact path, projected input/output tokens, projected total, threshold status, stop reason when blocked, cost estimate path when available, workflow-state budget summary, and maturity-report budget_status. Human-readable docs must keep the no-overclaim boundary clear: assisted_loop can proceed with documented limits; self_autonomous requires healthy budget evidence.
sections.7_execution_plan: Implement in six slices plus validation. First define budget-guard semantics. Second extend schemas and fixtures. Third add the loop-budget-check CLI/MCP contract. Fourth wire budget checks into action validation and workflow resume state. Fifth wire budget status into maturity audit and self-autonomy no-overclaim. Sixth update agent/skill/docs surfaces. Finally run full validation and leave the repo ready for a tech-debt cleanup pass.

8_task_tickets.title: Define Loop Budget Guard semantics
8_task_tickets.objective: Document the budget guard as part of ADLC loop-system maturity without creating a second budget framework.

decision_contract.resolution: Reuse existing token-budget, pre-turn-check, cost-reporting, and stop-reason specs; bind them into Loop Contracts only when LLM-backed loop behavior is active.

scope: Update docs/specs/loop-system-maturity-audit.md with budget_guard, budget_status, and budget_exhausted no-overclaim rules.
Update docs/specs/token-budgets.md and docs/specs/pre-turn-check.md only where needed to define loop-specific evidence refs.
Update README.md to state that budget evidence is required for self_autonomous loop claims.
Keep assisted_loop compatibility explicit.
out_of_scope: No provider billing API integration.
No new scheduler or hosted runner.
No required budget fields for deterministic docs/lint/build-validation work.

acceptance_criteria.given: a workflow claims self_autonomous loop maturity
acceptance_criteria.when: the loop-system maturity spec is read
acceptance_criteria.then: the spec requires healthy budget evidence as a no-overclaim gate
acceptance_criteria.measurable_post_condition: docs/specs/loop-system-maturity-audit.md names budget_guard, budget_status, budget_exhausted, and self_autonomous downgrade behavior

acceptance_criteria.given: a deterministic or assisted_loop task has no budget guard
acceptance_criteria.when: ADLC docs describe Loop Contract activation
acceptance_criteria.then: the task is not forced to add budget artifacts unless it delegates an LLM-backed loop action or claims self_autonomous
acceptance_criteria.measurable_post_condition: README.md and the loop maturity spec both state the compatibility boundary

anti_slop_rules: Do not add vague cost-awareness prose without machine-readable fields.
Do not claim budget controls are enforced before CLI/schema tasks implement them.
Do not make budget evidence mandatory for non-LLM deterministic tasks.

tech_debt_boundaries.prerequisite_debt: Budget specs exist outside loop maturity and can be missed by autonomous-loop claims.
tech_debt_boundaries.deferred_debt: Provider-specific billing reconciliation remains outside this slice.
tech_debt_boundaries.deferral_safety: Local token-budget artifacts and estimates are enough to block runaway loop claims without requiring provider integration.

compatibility_contract.backward: Existing Loop Contracts continue to validate until schema tasks add optional budget fields.
compatibility_contract.forward: The spec names stable budget_guard and budget_status concepts for schema and CLI implementation.
compatibility_contract.migration_or_rollout: Docs land first, then schema and CLI tasks make the contract executable.

implementation_notes: Keep the LLM-driven action, deterministic control-plane split intact.
Use stop reason budget_exhausted from docs/specs/stop-reasons.md.
Treat budget evidence as a maturity gate, not a blanket workflow tax.

primary_verifier.rationale: The budget guard semantics must be documented before schema or CLI work begins.
primary_verifier.expected_failure_mode: budget guard semantics missing from loop-system docs

evidence_responsibilities: Show the exact spec lines for budget_guard, budget_status, budget_exhausted, and self_autonomous downgrade.
Show that assisted_loop remains valid without budget evidence.
definition_of_done: Loop budget guard semantics are versioned in docs.
No-overclaim language is explicit.
No runtime enforcement is claimed before later tasks implement it.
failure_modes: MEDIUM: Budget guard becomes broad prose and cannot be tested; mitigation: name schema/CLI concepts up front.
MEDIUM: Existing workflows are made noisy; mitigation: optional until LLM-backed loop or self_autonomous claim.

invariants: Budget evidence is required only for active LLM-backed loop claims.
Docs do not claim enforcement before CLI/schema tasks land.

productionization_gate.claim: Budget guard semantics are defined but not yet enforced.
no_overclaim: Do not claim budget enforcement until ADLC-LBG-003 and ADLC-LBG-004 pass.
Do not claim self_autonomous support from docs alone.
reliability_failure_modes: Budget semantics drift from existing token-budget specs.

evidence: Docs require missing or unhealthy budget evidence to block self_autonomous claims.

8_task_tickets.title: Extend loop budget schemas and fixtures
8_task_tickets.objective: Add optional budget guard fields and fixtures across Loop Contract, Loop Action, Loop Maturity Report, workflow state, and token-budget validation aliases.

decision_contract.resolution: Extend existing schemas with backward-compatible optional fields; require them only by evaluator policy for self_autonomous claims.

scope: Add optional budget_guard to docs/schemas/loop-contract.schema.json.
Add optional budget_estimate to docs/schemas/loop-action.schema.json.
Add budget_status to docs/schemas/loop-maturity-report.schema.json.
Add optional budget_status or budget_events to docs/schemas/workflow-state.schema.json.
Add token-budget and session-state schema aliases in scripts/adlc_runtime/metadata.py.
Add healthy, warning, exhausted, and stale budget fixtures under tests/fixtures/loop_maturity/.
out_of_scope: No CLI enforcement in this task.
No provider billing reconciliation.
No mandatory migration of existing loop fixtures unless needed for report schema compatibility.

acceptance_criteria.given: a Loop Contract declares a token budget ref and hard stop behavior
acceptance_criteria.then: the contract, action, workflow-state, and maturity-report fixtures validate against schemas
acceptance_criteria.measurable_post_condition: validate-artifact passes for budgeted loop fixtures

acceptance_criteria.given: the existing assisted-loop fixtures do not carry a budget guard
acceptance_criteria.measurable_post_condition: tests/test_adlc_cli.sh and tests/test_adlc_contracts.sh keep existing loop fixture assertions green

anti_slop_rules: Do not duplicate token-budget.schema.json inside Loop Contract.
Do not make optional budget fields globally required.
Do not accept arbitrary unstructured budget blobs.

tech_debt_boundaries.prerequisite_debt: Loop schemas lack budget evidence fields and schema aliases for token-budget validation.
tech_debt_boundaries.deferred_debt: Real provider usage ingestion can be added after local budget semantics are stable.
tech_debt_boundaries.deferral_safety: Fixtures and schema aliases let the CLI enforce budget decisions deterministically now.

compatibility_contract.backward: Existing loop fixtures and Build Briefs validate without budget_guard unless self_autonomous is requested by evaluator logic.
compatibility_contract.forward: Budget fields reference token-budget artifacts by path so future runtime adapters can populate them.
compatibility_contract.migration_or_rollout: Add budgeted fixtures alongside existing fixtures; update existing reports only if schema requires budget_status with compatibility defaults.

implementation_notes: Prefer small objects with refs: token_budget_ref, cost_report_ref, max_projected_tokens, hard_stop_behavior, stale_after_seconds.
Keep budget_status reportable as healthy, warning, alert, exhausted, missing, or stale.
Schema alias names should be token-budget and session-state.

primary_verifier.rationale: Budgeted Loop Contracts must validate as structured artifacts.
primary_verifier.expected_failure_mode: budgeted loop contract fixture or schema field missing

secondary_verifiers.rationale: Token budget artifacts need a first-class schema alias.
Maturity reports must carry budget status evidence.
secondary_verifiers.expected_failure_mode: budget_status missing from report schema or fixture

evidence_responsibilities: Show budgeted fixtures validate.
Show existing assisted-loop fixtures still validate.
Show token-budget schema alias works.
definition_of_done: Budgeted loop fixtures exist and validate.
Existing loop fixtures remain valid.
Schema aliases cover token-budget and session-state.
failure_modes: HIGH: Schema change breaks existing assisted-loop fixtures; mitigation: keep fields optional or provide compatibility defaults.
MEDIUM: Budget evidence becomes free-form; mitigation: validate against token-budget schema and enumerated statuses.

invariants: Budget fields are optional for legacy assisted-loop fixtures.
Self-autonomous enforcement is implemented by evaluator policy, not by making all schemas globally strict.

productionization_gate.claim: Budget evidence can be represented as structured local artifacts.
no_overclaim: Schema support does not prove runtime budget enforcement.
Existing assisted-loop fixtures remain compatibility examples, not self-autonomous proof.

Budget fixtures pass but CLI does not consume them yet.

8_task_tickets.title: Add deterministic loop budget check CLI and MCP tool
8_task_tickets.objective: Implement the pre-turn budget decision as a deterministic ADLC command and expose it through MCP.

decision_contract.resolution: Implement local projected-token budget checks from token-budget artifacts; do not integrate provider billing APIs in this slice.

scope: Add bin/adlc loop-budget-check with --token-budget, --estimated-input-tokens, --expected-output-tokens, --phase, --skill, --json.
Return status proceed, warning, wrap_up, or blocked and include projected_total, threshold, budget_remaining, and stop_reason when blocked.
Exit nonzero only for blocked or invalid input.
Add command metadata and MCP tool schema.
Add CLI and MCP tests.
out_of_scope: No real-time provider usage pull.
No currency conversion.
No mutation of token-budget artifacts unless a later task explicitly adds it.

acceptance_criteria.given: a healthy token-budget artifact and a small projected action cost
acceptance_criteria.then: it returns proceed with projected totals and exits 0

acceptance_criteria.given: an exhausted token-budget artifact or projected cost crossing hard_stop_at
acceptance_criteria.then: it returns blocked with stop_reason budget_exhausted and exits nonzero

anti_slop_rules: Do not use LLM judgment for budget math.
Do not hide threshold math in prose-only output.
Do not treat warning and hard stop as the same state.

tech_debt_boundaries.prerequisite_debt: Budget checks are specified but not executable in the ADLC CLI.
tech_debt_boundaries.deferred_debt: Updating live token usage counters during real adapter execution can wait until runtime adapters own usage accounting.
tech_debt_boundaries.deferral_safety: A read-only deterministic command is enough for action admission and maturity evidence.

compatibility_contract.backward: Existing CLI commands and MCP tools remain unchanged.
compatibility_contract.forward: The output shape can be embedded in action validation, workflow state, and maturity reports.
compatibility_contract.migration_or_rollout: Add the command and tests without making callers use it until later tasks wire it in.

implementation_notes: Use integer token math and threshold ratios from token-budget.schema.json artifacts.
Map alert_at to wrap_up, hard_stop_at to blocked, and warn_at to warning.
Include phase and skill only as attribution fields, not as required budget lookup keys.

primary_verifier.rationale: ADLC must expose a deterministic budget decision command.

secondary_verifiers.rationale: CLI tests must cover proceed, warning or wrap_up, and blocked budget outcomes.
secondary_verifiers.expected_failure_mode: budget CLI tests missing or failing

secondary_verifiers.rationale: MCP clients need the same budget-check tool.

verification_spec.scope_note: Read-only budget-check command and MCP exposure.

evidence_responsibilities: Show proceed and blocked JSON outputs.
Show blocked output includes stop_reason budget_exhausted.
Show MCP tools/list includes adlc_loop_budget_check.
definition_of_done: loop-budget-check exists and is deterministic.
MCP exposes the same command.
CLI tests cover healthy and exhausted budget paths.
failure_modes: HIGH: Hard-stop math allows a projected overrun; mitigation: test crossing hard_stop_at.
MEDIUM: Command returns ambiguous status; mitigation: enum status and stop_reason assertions.

invariants: Warning and wrap_up states remain distinguishable.

productionization_gate.claim: ADLC can compute local loop budget decisions before LLM-backed actions.
no_overclaim: The command estimates projected budget state; it does not pull provider billing usage.
The command does not enforce action admission until ADLC-LBG-004 wires it in.
reliability_failure_modes: Projected cost is missing or underestimated by caller.

evidence: Hard-stop budget state returns budget_exhausted before another LLM turn.
Budget decision JSON includes thresholds and projected totals.

8_task_tickets.title: Gate loop action admission and resume state with budget evidence
8_task_tickets.objective: Wire budget checks into LLM action admission and workflow resume summaries so budget exhaustion can stop the loop before another action executes.

decision_contract.resolution: Action admission may consume a budget artifact when provided; missing budget evidence is handled by maturity policy in ADLC-LBG-005.

scope: Extend loop-action-validate with optional --token-budget or budget refs from the Loop Contract.
Reject or route actions when loop-budget-check returns blocked.
Add budget_status to workflow-state fixture and resume-workflow next_action output.
Preserve existing action-validation behavior when no token budget is provided for assisted_loop contracts.
out_of_scope: No live process kill switch.
No provider API usage sync.
No mutation of token-budget artifacts during validation.

acceptance_criteria.given: a loop action would cross the hard-stop budget threshold
acceptance_criteria.then: the action is rejected or routed with budget_exhausted before execution
next_action includes the budget status and stop reason when present

anti_slop_rules: Do not execute or simulate the LLM action after a blocked budget state.
Do not make no-budget assisted_loop validation fail in this task.
Do not bury budget reasons inside generic rejected-action text.

tech_debt_boundaries.prerequisite_debt: loop-action-validate enforces tools, tests, controls, and checkpoints but not budget state.
tech_debt_boundaries.deferred_debt: Runtime adapters can later write live usage into token-budget artifacts.
tech_debt_boundaries.deferral_safety: Read-only action admission still prevents another loop step when supplied evidence says budget is exhausted.

compatibility_contract.backward: Existing loop-action-validate invocations without --token-budget keep their current assisted_loop behavior.
compatibility_contract.forward: Budget output shape is reusable by runtime adapters and MCP callers.
compatibility_contract.migration_or_rollout: Add optional arguments and state fields, then update tests and docs.

implementation_notes: Reuse loop_budget_check_payload rather than reimplementing budget math.
Return a concrete issue rule such as budget_exhausted when blocking.
Resume output should report budget_status without modifying unrelated workflow behavior.

primary_verifier.rationale: Action admission must surface budget exhaustion before execution.
primary_verifier.expected_failure_mode: loop-action-validate does not accept or enforce token-budget evidence

secondary_verifiers.rationale: Resume output must surface budget state for continuing loops.

verification_spec.scope_note: Action admission and resume-state budget integration.

evidence_responsibilities: Show budget-exhausted action validation output.
Show legacy action validation still admits existing valid-loop-action fixture.
Show resume-workflow includes budget_status.
definition_of_done: Budget evidence can block loop action admission.
Workflow resume output includes budget status.
Existing action-validation tests remain green.
failure_modes: HIGH: Action runs despite exhausted budget; mitigation: CLI rejection test.
MEDIUM: Budget option breaks legacy callers; mitigation: existing assisted-loop action tests remain unchanged.

invariants: Legacy assisted-loop action validation remains compatible.

productionization_gate.claim: ADLC can use budget evidence to block an LLM-backed loop action before execution.
no_overclaim: This is state/action admission, not live process interruption.
Provider usage collection remains outside this slice.
reliability_failure_modes: Caller omits budget evidence for assisted_loop actions.

evidence: Budget-exhausted actions are rejected or routed before execution.

8_task_tickets.title: Make budget evidence part of loop maturity and self-autonomy no-overclaim
8_task_tickets.objective: Extend loop-maturity-audit so budget status is reported and missing or unhealthy budget evidence blocks self_autonomous verdicts.

decision_contract.resolution: Budget evidence is a no-overclaim gate for self_autonomous loops, not a requirement for assisted_loop compatibility.

scope: Add --token-budget or budget evidence input to loop-maturity-audit.
Emit budget_status in generated maturity reports.
Block self_autonomous verdict when budget evidence is missing, stale, warning, alert, or exhausted.
Keep assisted_loop verdict valid for legacy fixtures with clear missing budget mechanism.
Add tests for healthy budget self-autonomy path and missing/exhausted budget downgrade path.
out_of_scope: No model-provider bill reconciliation.
No automatic budget ref discovery beyond declared contract/state refs unless simple and deterministic.
No broad maturity rubric rewrite beyond budget status integration.

acceptance_criteria.given: a loop maturity audit has token-budget evidence
acceptance_criteria.then: the report includes budget_status with evidence refs and projected budget state
acceptance_criteria.measurable_post_condition: generated report validates against loop-maturity-report schema and includes budget_status

acceptance_criteria.given: a contract requests self_autonomous but has missing or exhausted budget evidence
acceptance_criteria.then: the verdict is not self_autonomous and prioritized gaps name the budget guard

anti_slop_rules: Do not let self_autonomous pass with missing budget evidence.
Do not count budget docs as runtime budget evidence.
Do not lower existing critical gates for win condition, test selection, or failure handling.

tech_debt_boundaries.prerequisite_debt: loop-maturity-audit scores seven dimensions but does not include budget state.
tech_debt_boundaries.deferred_debt: A richer eighth maturity dimension can be considered later if budget evidence grows beyond guard status.
tech_debt_boundaries.deferral_safety: A budget_status gate is enough to block overclaim without destabilizing the existing seven-dimension rubric.

compatibility_contract.backward: Existing assisted-loop maturity reports validate with missing budget status only if schema compatibility is preserved or fixtures are updated with non-blocking missing status.
compatibility_contract.forward: Future workflows can use healthy budget evidence as one requirement for self_autonomous maturity.
compatibility_contract.migration_or_rollout: Update fixtures and tests together so no stale report shape remains.

implementation_notes: Prefer budget_status as a top-level report field rather than an eighth score unless schema design proves otherwise.
If budget evidence is warning or wrap_up, self_autonomous should be blocked even if action execution can continue in assisted mode.
Prioritized gaps should name budget evidence clearly.

primary_verifier.rationale: Maturity reports must include budget evidence when supplied.
primary_verifier.expected_failure_mode: loop-maturity-audit does not accept or emit token-budget evidence

secondary_verifiers.rationale: CLI tests must cover self-autonomous budget no-overclaim behavior.

evidence_responsibilities: Show healthy budget maturity output includes budget_status.
Show missing or exhausted budget evidence blocks self_autonomous.
Show existing assisted_loop report remains truthful.
definition_of_done: loop-maturity-audit emits budget_status.
Self-autonomous claims require healthy budget evidence.
Existing no-overclaim gates still block weak win condition, test selection, and failure handling.
failure_modes: HIGH: Self-autonomous verdict passes with missing budget guard; mitigation: explicit downgrade test.
MEDIUM: Budget status obscures existing seven-dimension gaps; mitigation: preserve existing score logic and prioritized gaps.

invariants: Missing or unhealthy budget evidence blocks self_autonomous.

productionization_gate.claim: Self-autonomous loop maturity requires healthy budget guard evidence.
no_overclaim: Healthy local budget evidence is necessary but not sufficient for self_autonomous maturity.
reliability_failure_modes: Runtime adapter does not write live token usage yet.

evidence: Maturity audit blocks self_autonomous when budget evidence is missing or unhealthy.

8_task_tickets.title: Wire budget guards into agents, skills, docs, and learning
8_task_tickets.objective: Make budget guards discoverable and required by ADLC planning/review surfaces when loop autonomy or LLM-backed loop actions are active.

decision_contract.resolution: Wire budget guard expectations into existing ADLC agents and skills rather than adding a new planning framework.

scope: Update agents/planner.md to require budget_guard refs for self_autonomous or LLM-backed loop claims.
Update skills/codegen-context/SKILL.md to pass compact budget refs and budget_status, not raw logs.
Update skills/eval-council/SKILL.md to block missing budget evidence for self_autonomous loop claims.
Update skills/llm-security/SKILL.md and docs/specs/agent-native-interface.md to name loop-budget-check and budget_exhausted stop reasons.
Update docs/specs/compound-engineering-learning-store.md so captured loop learnings preserve budget_status evidence and stale conditions.
Update README verification examples and tests.
out_of_scope: No new large skill bundle.
No automatic scheduling or automation setup.
No raw prompt or raw command-output capture in learning entries.

acceptance_criteria.given: a planner or Eval Council sees a self_autonomous loop claim
acceptance_criteria.when: budget_guard refs are absent or unhealthy
acceptance_criteria.then: the task is revised or blocked with a budget-specific reason
acceptance_criteria.measurable_post_condition: agent/skill docs contain budget_guard, budget_status, loop-budget-check, and budget_exhausted rules

acceptance_criteria.given: a verified loop pattern is captured into compound learning
acceptance_criteria.then: the learning entry preserves budget_status evidence and stale conditions without raw logs
acceptance_criteria.measurable_post_condition: compound learning docs name budget_status evidence refs and redaction constraints

anti_slop_rules: Do not paste full budget reports or command logs into codegen context.
Do not make budget guards a generic checklist item for non-loop work.
Do not add a new planner or review stack.

tech_debt_boundaries.prerequisite_debt: Agent/skill guidance knows Loop Contracts but not budget guard evidence.
tech_debt_boundaries.deferred_debt: A dedicated automation/runbook skill can wait until repeated scheduled ADLC loops need it.
tech_debt_boundaries.deferral_safety: Existing planner, codegen-context, Eval Council, and learning-capture surfaces are enough to make budget evidence usable.

compatibility_contract.backward: Existing non-loop tasks remain unaffected by budget guard guidance.
compatibility_contract.forward: Future automation or runtime adapter skills can reuse the same budget_guard and budget_status vocabulary.
compatibility_contract.migration_or_rollout: Docs and skills update alongside CLI/schema tests.

implementation_notes: Prefer compact refs: token_budget_ref, cost_report_ref, budget_status, and verifier command.
Use explicit block reason missing_loop_budget_guard or budget_guard_unhealthy where appropriate.
Keep learning capture evidence-based and redacted.

primary_verifier.target: rg -n "budget_guard|budget_status|loop-budget-check|budget_exhausted|self_autonomous" agents/planner.md skills/codegen-context/SKILL.md skills/eval-council/SKILL.md skills/llm-security/SKILL.md docs/specs/agent-native-interface.md docs/specs/compound-engineering-learning-store.md README.md
primary_verifier.rationale: Core ADLC agents, skills, and docs must consume budget guard evidence.
primary_verifier.expected_failure_mode: budget guard guidance missing from agents or skills

secondary_verifiers.rationale: Contract tests must lock budget guard wiring into docs and skills.
secondary_verifiers.expected_failure_mode: contract tests do not cover budget guard wiring

evidence_responsibilities: Show rg coverage across planner, codegen-context, Eval Council, LLM security, agent-native docs, learning store, and README.
Show contract tests cover the new guidance.
Show learning capture guidance avoids raw budget logs.
definition_of_done: Agents and skills know when budget guards are required.
Eval Council can block unsupported self-autonomous budget claims.
Compound learning can capture budget-aware loop patterns with redacted evidence refs.
failure_modes: MEDIUM: Budget guard exists but agents ignore it; mitigation: planner/Eval Council/codegen contract tests.
MEDIUM: Learning entries capture raw logs; mitigation: redaction and compact-ref guidance.

implementation_interface_contract.capability: Agent and skill consumption of loop budget evidence
invariants: Prompts receive compact budget refs, not raw logs.
Self-autonomous loop claims require budget evidence.

productionization_gate.claim: ADLC planning, context, review, and learning surfaces consume budget guard evidence.
no_overclaim: Agent guidance does not create live billing enforcement.
Budget-aware learning entries are evidence refs, not proof of future budget health.
reliability_failure_modes: Prompt bloat from raw budget reports.
Eval Council misses missing budget guard claims.

evidence: Planner, codegen, Eval Council, and learning docs preserve budget evidence refs.

8_task_tickets.title: Validate loop budget guard integration end to end
8_task_tickets.objective: Run deterministic validation for the full budget-guard integration and leave evidence for the follow-on tech-debt cleanup pass.

decision_contract.resolution: Validation task only; no Type 1 decision is required.

scope: Run schema validation for this Build Brief and all budgeted loop fixtures.
Run loop-budget-check healthy, warning or wrap_up, and exhausted paths.
Run loop-action-validate with budget evidence.
Run loop-maturity-audit with budget evidence and self-autonomy downgrade cases.
Run contract tests, CLI tests, Python compile, diff hygiene, and Graphify update/query.
Document any smoke blocker and exact rerun path.
out_of_scope: No broad tech-debt refactor in this validation task.
No PR publish/merge unless explicitly requested after validation.

acceptance_criteria.then: schema, CLI, contract, maturity, and budget guard tests pass
acceptance_criteria.measurable_post_condition: tests/test_adlc_cli.sh and tests/test_adlc_contracts.sh pass with budget guard coverage

acceptance_criteria.then: the graph can describe the budget guard integration and any remaining risks
acceptance_criteria.measurable_post_condition: graphify update . succeeds and a budget guard query returns relevant nodes

anti_slop_rules: Do not mark complete if generated fixtures or reports are dirty without explanation.
Do not claim self_autonomous framework status from local budget guard tests alone.
Do not skip Graphify update after code/doc changes.

tech_debt_boundaries.prerequisite_debt: Implementation may leave runtime modularity or naming cleanup opportunities.
tech_debt_boundaries.deferred_debt: Run a dedicated tech-debt cleanup pass after this validation task rather than mixing broad refactors into implementation.
tech_debt_boundaries.deferral_safety: Validation evidence and Graphify output give the tech-debt pass a clean target.

compatibility_contract.backward: Existing ADLC loop, compound, emitter, schema, and smoke-harness contract tests remain green.
compatibility_contract.forward: Budget guard evidence is now available for future automation/runbook or runtime-adapter work.
compatibility_contract.migration_or_rollout: Ship as opt-in budget guards for LLM-backed loops; do not require every Build Brief to change.

implementation_notes: Restore generated timestamp-only artifacts such as tests/backtest/last_report.json before closeout unless intentionally changed.
If smoke cannot run because credentials or adapter config are absent, record the exact blocker and deterministic validations instead.
Leave a concise follow-on tech-debt cleanup target list.

primary_verifier.rationale: The full decomposition must remain readiness-clean after implementation.

secondary_verifiers.rationale: The Build Brief must remain schema-valid.
Contract and CLI behavior must remain green.

verification_spec.scope_note: End-to-end validation for budget guard integration.

evidence_responsibilities: Record exact validation commands and pass/fail results.
Record final maturity verdict and budget_status behavior.
Record Graphify update/query outcome.
Record tech-debt cleanup targets without doing broad cleanup in this validation task.
definition_of_done: All deterministic validation commands pass or blockers are documented with rerun paths.
Current loop maturity remains assisted_loop by default unless a specific fixture legitimately earns self_autonomous.
Follow-on tech-debt cleanup scope is clear.
failure_modes: HIGH: Validation passes but self-autonomy is overclaimed; mitigation: explicit no-overclaim closeout.
MEDIUM: Generated artifacts dirty the worktree; mitigation: inspect and restore unintentional generated churn.

implementation_interface_contract.reason: Validation closeout task; implementation interfaces are covered by the implementation tasks.

productionization_gate.claim: Loop budget guard integration is validated for local ADLC artifacts and deterministic CLI/MCP contracts.
no_overclaim: Do not claim live kill-switch support.
reliability_failure_modes: Budget evidence is local and can become stale.
Runtime adapters do not yet write live provider token usage.

evidence: Validation records CLI, maturity, budget, and Graphify evidence.

9_risks: Budget evidence could become stale if runtime adapters do not write live usage; mitigate with stale status and self_autonomous downgrade.
Schema changes could break existing loop fixtures; mitigate by keeping budget fields optional for assisted_loop compatibility.
Budget controls could be mistaken for real billing enforcement; mitigate with no-overclaim gates and explicit local-estimate wording.
Runtime CLI could grow denser; defer broad modularity cleanup to the follow-on tech-debt pass.
sections.10_rollout: Ship as an opt-in loop budget guard overlay. Existing assisted_loop workflows continue unchanged. New or updated LLM-backed loop tasks can add budget_guard refs immediately. self_autonomous claims require healthy budget_status evidence after this slice.
11_validation_plan: python3 -m py_compile scripts/adlc.py scripts/adlc_runtime/__init__.py scripts/adlc_runtime/metadata.py scripts/adlc_runtime/cli.py scripts/validate_learning_entry.py
graphify query "How does ADLC bind Loop Contracts to token budget guards, action admission, maturity reports, workflow state, and self-autonomy no-overclaim?" --budget 3000

12_skill_trigger_configuration.trigger: A task delegates decisions, tool use, retry/repair, or maturity claims to an LLM-backed loop.
12_skill_trigger_configuration.required_change: Require budget_guard refs for self_autonomous claims and name loop-budget-check as a verifier when budget evidence is active.

12_skill_trigger_configuration.trigger: A coding task has active loop_contract_path and budget_guard refs.
12_skill_trigger_configuration.required_change: Pass compact token_budget_ref, cost_report_ref, and budget_status evidence, not raw token logs.

12_skill_trigger_configuration.trigger: A maturity or productionization claim references self_autonomous loop behavior.
12_skill_trigger_configuration.required_change: Block missing_loop_budget_guard or budget_guard_unhealthy before approving self_autonomous claims.

12_skill_trigger_configuration.trigger: A task introduces or changes LLM-backed loop execution.
12_skill_trigger_configuration.required_change: Require token/cost budget evidence and structured budget_exhausted stop reasons.

12_skill_trigger_configuration.trigger: Learning capture stores a verified loop pattern.
12_skill_trigger_configuration.required_change: Capture budget_status refs and stale conditions for LLM-backed loop learnings.

14_graph_research_evidence.preflight_query: graphify query "How should loop budget guards integrate with Loop Contracts, token budgets, action admission, maturity reports, workflow state, and no-overclaim gates?" --budget 3000
14_graph_research_evidence.note: graphify update . is AST-only in this local CLI; semantic doc refresh requires graphify extract with an LLM backend.

15_dark_code_risk.risk: scripts/adlc_runtime/cli.py is already large; the implementation should keep budget logic factored into small helpers so the tech-debt cleanup pass can split modules without behavior changes.
15_dark_code_risk.mitigation: Add focused helper functions and tests now; defer broad runtime modularity cleanup to the follow-on tech-debt pass.

16_implementation_interfaces.capability: Budget guard evidence across Loop Contract, Loop Action, workflow state, maturity report, CLI/MCP, and agent guidance
invariants: Budget evidence is required for self_autonomous LLM-backed loops.
Budget math is deterministic and schema-backed.
Provider billing integration is not claimed.
16_implementation_interfaces.privacy_redaction: Budget evidence stores counts, thresholds, status, and refs only; it must not store prompts, keys, bearer tokens, or billing account identifiers.

17_productionization_gates.claim: Budget guard evidence is a necessary no-overclaim gate for self_autonomous LLM-backed loops.
no_overclaim: Healthy local budget evidence is necessary but not sufficient for self_autonomous maturity.
Provider billing reconciliation is not implemented.
Live kill-switch support is not implemented.
Global ADLC self-autonomy is not claimed.
known_unsupported_states: live signal interruption of already-running actions
reliability_failure_modes: Projected token estimates can be low.
Runtime adapters may not yet write live usage.

security_privacy.threat_model_delta: Budget artifacts add local cost/usage metadata but no secrets or external auth.
security_privacy.secrets_handling: Do not persist prompts, API keys, bearer tokens, or billing account IDs.
security_privacy.data_retention: Budget artifacts are repo-local fixtures or workflow/session state refs.
security_privacy.redaction_posture: Store counts, costs, model IDs, statuses, and evidence refs only.
